output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Get It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and demand your money back fast with Deposits-Back.com. No signup required.
content_outline: Why Landlords Withhold Security Deposits (And Hope You Give Up)
What Landlords Can Legally Deduct vs. Normal Wear and Tear
State Deadlines: How Long Is Too Long?
The 60-Second Demand Letter That Actually Works
What to Do If Your Landlord Still Refuses to Pay
key_points: Landlords often withhold deposits illegally assuming tenants won't fight back
State laws limit deductions and impose strict return deadlines—often 14 to 30 days
Normal wear and tear, such as minor scuffs and faded paint, is not a valid deduction
A formal letter of demand is the fastest, cheapest way to force compliance before small claims court
Deposits-Back.com generates a legally sound demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99 with no subscription or signup
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup, no subscription, no stress.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters dealing with landlords who are wrongfully withholding, deducting from, or failing to return a security deposit
tiktok_reel_hooks: POV: Your landlord is withholding your $2,000 deposit for 'cleaning' but you left the place spotless 🚩

Your landlord is banking on the fact that you don't know these 3 tenant rights... 💀

short_form_scripts.title: The Myth Buster
short_form_scripts.hook: Your landlord CANNOT keep your deposit for normal wear and tear.
short_form_scripts.script:
Scuffed floors? Nope.
Small nail holes? Nope.
'Deep cleaning' when you left it clean? Absolutely not.
If they're withholding it illegally, hit them with a formal demand letter.
Deposits-Back dot com. 60 seconds. No signup.
